title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs reader outcomes, repository baseline, static architecture, and evidence from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
Inspect status, revision, configuration origin, ignores, content licenses, generated files, and existing gates before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Reader Outcome · changed case · Review Reader Outcome
steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 9508 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence. Artifact under review: reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Evidence Ladder, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Evidence Ladder: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 7889 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Evidence Ladder precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review publishing team handles case 7889 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A visually correct local page proves the published site is complete.
